problem:
For each positive integer $n$, let $n!$ denote the product $1\cdot 2\cdot 3\cdot\,\cdots\,\cdot (n-1)\cdot n$.

What is the remainder when $9!$ is divided by $11$?

classified_questions.question: How can we calculate the remainder when a number is divided by another number?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Can we use modular arithmetic to simplify the calculation of $9!$ modulo $11$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Is there a pattern in the remainders when smaller factorials are divided by $11$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we use modular arithmetic to simplify the calculation of $n!$ modulo a prime number $p$?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Are there any other properties of factorials that are useful for number theory problems?
classified_questions.label: e
